run_mode_note: Re-run with v1-strict-mode-aware prompt. Tests whether updating the prompt to surface v1 strict patterns as first-class output requirements pulls scores back up to the 1.0 calibration baseline (which the strict-items-only re-judging on the v0 prompt landed at 0.952).
v0_to_v1_prompt_delta: v0 prompt run scored 1.000 on v0 rubric (0.952 on v1 rubric via strict-item re-judge). v1 prompt run scores 1.000 on v1 rubric — the prompt update successfully pulls strong analyses back up to the calibration baseline because it surfaces structural-reason, flip-threshold, and non-flagged-mode-justification as required output sections.

item_scores.reasoning: Single TALC stage decisively (Innovators/Early Adopters in Brazilian market). Multi-evidence: technical-founder-led startups, innovation-lab pilots, no pragmatist procurement, no Portuguese collateral. 'Pulled by enthusiasts, not pushing into pragmatist procurement' — explicit visionary framing.

'Before the chasm' decisive. Reasoning explicitly contrasts visionary motivations (frontier capability, technical-founder taste, innovation-lab experimentation) against pragmatist motivations (LGPD attestation, BRL contracting, local presence, named references). Multi-vendor pilots without standardization cited as definitional pre-chasm state.

Heads of AI / Chief Data Officers at Brazilian digital-native fintechs (Nubank, Stone, PagSeguro, Inter, XP, BTG), 1,000-10,000 employees, São Paulo HQ. Role + named industry + size band + geography + named example logos. Single beachhead with rationale tied to pragmatist-within-visionary-segment framing.

7 concrete named items: LGPD compliance attestation (named cert), Brazilian data residency with Anthropic-direct contractual privity, named Brazilian fintech reference (Nubank/Stone/Inter case study with named exec on record), BRL-denominated contracts, Portuguese-language enterprise sales motion (local AE, SE, CS, legal/procurement in São Paulo), Brazilian fintech stack integrations (named: KYC pipelines, banking middleware), forward-looking ANPD AI-guidance readiness statement.

3 pins with explicit reference-value chaining: (1) digital fintechs (beachhead), (2) incumbent retail banks (Itaú, Bradesco — fintech reference unlocks bank conversation because banks benchmark themselves against Nubank stack), (3) retail/telco (Magazine Luiza, Vivo, TIM — track what banks adopt). Plus explicit deferral of public-sector/SOEs as 'different go-to-market motion, not adjacent pin.'

3 strong-evidence modes flagged with citations: whole product blindness (no Brazil sales team, no SP office, no Portuguese, no BRL pricing — textbook whole-product blindness stranding vendor on visionary side), confused TALC stage (cites expansion thesis treating flagship customers as parallel to Portuguese polish — should be sequenced), parallel pursuit mild (cites 'fintech, AI-native consumer, enterprise innovation labs, public sector, broader LATAM' all in one breath).

Dedicated section. 'Low hundreds of paying Brazilian customers and tens of thousands of Claude.ai subscribers represent continued visionary and innovator penetration, not crossing.' Names what crossing would look like (named Brazilian regulated-industry customer publicly standardizing, procurement-gated LGPD attestation, pilot-to-production conversion at banks). Calls out the cost of mistaking visionary self-serve for early-majority traction.

TALC 8/10, Chasm 8/10 — both as integers. 3 specific named-metric open questions: pilot-to-production conversion rate at 4 named banks, Anthropic-Brazilian-entity LGPD-direct contract status (binary trigger), Portuguese-vs-English Claude API usage ratio from Brazilian IPs (>40% threshold makes Portuguese binding, <15% makes it deferrable).

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Whole Product Gap section names the structural reason existing alternatives fail: 'Bedrock-mediated access makes AWS the contractual counterparty for the Brazilian buyer; Anthropic is, from the procurement office's perspective, a sub-processor named in an AWS addendum. Pragmatist Brazilian fintech buyers under LGPD enforcement need the model maker itself as the named counterparty... This is a different counterparty type, not a different feature — Anthropic-direct LGPD attestation is structurally unavailable through a hyperscaler reseller channel.' Names the structural property (counterparty type) and what existing solutions structurally cannot match.

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Open Questions section names quantitative flip-thresholds with explicit flip-direction: 'if pilot-to-production conversion at the named banks exceeds 20% within 12 months of pilot start, chasm position shifts from before the chasm to approaching the chasm... If conversion sits below 5%, position hardens to before the chasm and the beachhead must narrow further.' Two flip-thresholds with two flip-directions named.

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Dedicated 'failure modes explicitly NOT flagged, with reasons' subsection. Wrong beachhead not flagged: 'This diagnostic is recommending a beachhead; the inputs do not show Anthropic having committed to a beachhead choice yet. To flag wrong-beachhead, the inputs would need to show a stated, resourced commitment to a specific named segment.' Skipping the bowling pin not flagged: 'To flag this, the inputs would need to show Anthropic publicly announcing or executing a all-of-LATAM-enterprise-simultaneously plan. The inputs show the opposite — there is no plan yet, only a thesis.'
